prompt: List every AI startup that raised a round of $300M+ OR crossed a $5B valuation in 2026 (January-present, today is June 2026). For each: company name, round size, valuation, date, one-line description, category. Be exhaustive and specific. Cite sources (TechCrunch, Crunchbase, Bloomberg, The Information, X posts).
content:
**Several AI startups met the criteria (raised ≥$300M in a round or crossed $5B valuation) from January to early June 2026.** This is based on reported deals from sources like Crunchbase, TechCrunch, Bloomberg, company announcements, and related coverage; it focuses on the most prominent and well-documented cases, as exhaustive real-time tracking of every minor or undisclosed deal is challenging.[[1]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/biggest-funding-rounds-ai-robotics-ecommerce-quince/)[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)

Here is a compiled list (not necessarily exhaustive of every niche or unreported deal, but covering major reported ones):

- **xAI** — $20B Series E (Jan 6, 2026); valuation ~$230B. One-line description: Elon Musk-founded company building Grok models and massive AI compute infrastructure. Category: Frontier/generative AI.[[3]](https://x.ai/news/series-e)[[4]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/biggest-funding-rounds-xai-parabilis-medicines-soley-therapeutics/)

- **OpenAI** — $122B funding round (Mar 2026); valuation $730B–$840B+. One-line description: Leading developer of frontier AI models including ChatGPT and related products. Category: Frontier AI.[[5]](https://www.designrush.com/agency/business-consulting/trends/ai-startup-funding-stages)[[6]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/record-breaking-funding-ai-global-q1-2026/)

- **Anthropic** — $30B round (early 2026) and $65B Series H (May 28, 2026); valuation rose to $380B then $965B. One-line description: Safety-focused AI lab behind Claude models and enterprise tools. Category: Frontier AI/safety.[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)[[5]](https://www.designrush.com/agency/business-consulting/trends/ai-startup-funding-stages)

- **World Labs** (Fei-Fei Li) — $1B round (Feb 18, 2026); valuation ~$5B. One-line description: Building spatial intelligence and 3D world models (e.g., Marble platform). Category: Spatial/embodied AI.[[7]](https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog/funding-2026)[[8]](https://www.thesaasnews.com/news/world-labs-raises-1b-series-b/)

- **Cognition AI** — >$1B Series D (May 27, 2026); valuation $26B (up from $10.2B). One-line description: Developer of Devin, an autonomous AI software engineer/agent for coding. Category: Agentic AI/coding.[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)[[9]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/27/ai-coding-startup-cognition-raises-1b-at-25b-pre-money-valuation/)

- **Etched** — $500M Series A (Jan 13, 2026); valuation $5B. One-line description: AI chip startup focused on transformer models and specialized silicon. Category: AI hardware/semiconductors.[[10]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/homen-shum_startups-funding-venturecapital-activity-7417045813329301504-3S9t)

- **Supabase** — $500M round (June 2026); valuation $10.5B. One-line description: Open-source backend/database platform optimized for building AI applications. Category: AI infrastructure/tooling.[[11]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/04/database-startup-supabase-raises-500-million-10point5-billion-valuation.html)

- **Sierra** — $950M Series E (May 4, 2026); valuation $15.8B. One-line description: Bret Taylor-founded platform for enterprise AI agents in customer experience. Category: Agentic AI/enterprise.[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)

- **Nexthop AI** — $500M Series B (~Mar 2026); valuation ~$4.2B post-money. One-line description: AI networking/switching technology for data centers and cloud/AI clusters. Category: AI infrastructure/networking.[[1]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/biggest-funding-rounds-ai-robotics-ecommerce-quince/)

- **Mind Robotics** — $500M Series A (~Mar 2026). One-line description: Industrial AI robotics platform (Rivian spinout) for manufacturing automation. Category: Robotics/AI automation.[[1]](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/biggest-funding-rounds-ai-robotics-ecommerce-quince/)

- **Flourish** — $500M round (early June 2026); valuation ~$2.5B–$3.3B. One-line description: Brain-inspired/neuromorphic AI systems (Cortex AI) emphasizing energy efficiency. Category: Neuromorphic/efficiency AI.[[12]](https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/ai-startup-flourish-reportedly-raises-500m-round-backed-jeff-bezos/)

- **Recursive Superintelligence (Ricursive Intelligence)** — $500M pre-Series A (Apr 2026); valuation ~$4B. One-line description: Advanced AI chip design or superintelligence-focused lab. Category: AI hardware/frontier.[[13]](https://www.cxodigitalpulse.com/ai-startup-recursive-superintelligence-secures-500m-pre-series-a/)

- **Anduril Industries** — $5B Series H (May 13, 2026); valuation $61B. One-line description: Autonomous defense systems and Lattice AI platform for military applications. Category: Defense AI/autonomous systems.[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)

Additional mentions of $300M+ rounds in the period (with less granular public details on exact dates/valuations in some cases) include Mach Industries ($300M Series D, defense/autonomous systems) and scattered reports of others like Ayar Labs ($500M range in infrastructure).[[14]](https://www.startuphub.ai/investment_rounds/mach-industries-series-d-2026)[[15]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/manishchoudhary-flexprice_300-billion-went-into-startups-in-q1-2026-activity-7448436783811436544-dIy5)

**Notes**: Valuations and round sizes are post-money where specified and drawn from contemporaneous reporting; some smaller or later-stage deals may exist but lack the same visibility. Big frontier labs (xAI, OpenAI, Anthropic) dominated total capital. Many infrastructure, agentic, hardware, and vertical AI plays also saw large rounds. Sources primarily include Crunchbase roundups, TechCrunch, Bloomberg, company blogs, and AI funding trackers. For the absolute latest or private deals, direct Crunchbase/ PitchBook checks or primary announcements are recommended.
